sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Newcastle United, sitting at 4th in the league with 63 points, host struggling Ipswich Town, who are 18th with just 22 points, in Round 34 at St James' Park in Newcastle upon Tyne. This matchup sees a stark contrast in form and league standing. The referee for the game, Michael Salisbury, averages 3.2 yellow cards and 0.20 red cards per game, suggesting a moderate disciplinary approach. Newcastle will look to capitalize on their home advantage in front of a passionate crowd. Meanwhile, Ipswich Town face an uphill battle to secure points in their fight against relegation.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last encounter on December 21, 2024, Newcastle United dominated Ipswich Town with a 0-4 victory away from home. Alexander Isak was the standout, earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 9.6. Over the last 5 matchups between these teams, Newcastle have secured 3 wins, with 1 draw and 1 win for Ipswich. Historically, matches between these sides have seen high-scoring affairs, with more than 2.5 goals in all of the last 6 head-to-head games. Jacob Murphy has been a consistent performer against Ipswich, averaging a Sofascore rating of 7.4, scoring 2 goals, and providing 1 assist in these fixtures.

sections.subtitle: Newcastle United's Form and Key Players
sections.text: Newcastle United are in fine form, with a streak of scoring over 2.5 goals in their last 6 home matches and being the first to score in 7 of their last 8 games. They’ve scored 66 goals this season, ranking 3rd in the league, and have created 101 big chances, placing them 5th. Star forward Alexander Isak leads the charge with 23 goals, ranking 2nd in the league, and a total expected goals value of 19.95. Jacob Murphy, with 11 assists (2nd in the league), and Harvey Barnes, in form with 4 goals and 2 assists in the last 5 matches, are also pivotal. Bruno Guimarães, ranking 1st in the league for times fouled per game at 2.9, often draws fouls to disrupt opposition play. However, they’ve underperformed their expected goals by -5.3373, ranking 15th in this metric, showing room for improvement in finishing.

sections.subtitle: Ipswich Town's Struggles and Standout Performers
sections.text: Ipswich Town are in poor shape, with no wins in their last 3 matches and without a clean sheet in 15 consecutive games. They’ve conceded goals consistently, with both teams scoring in 8 of their last 10 away matches, and over 2.5 goals in their last 5 away fixtures. Their attack is led by Liam Delap, who has scored 12 goals with an expected goals value of 9.27, showing clinical finishing. Defender Cameron Burgess stands out defensively, ranking 5th in the league for ground duels won at 73.1%. Players like Omari Hutchinson and Julio Enciso offer some creative spark with a combined 5 goals and 5 assists, but the team’s overall form remains a concern as they face a tough test at St James’ Park.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Newcastle United dominate in several statistical categories, ranking 3rd in clean sheets with 12 and 2nd in big chance conversion rate at 42.57%. They average 5.6 corners per game (8th in the league) and excel in fast breaks, ranking 3rd with 1.34 per game. Their goal distribution shows strength early and late, with 20% of goals scored in the first 15 minutes and another 20% between the 76th and 90th minutes. Ipswich Town, on the other hand, have struggled defensively, with opponents creating 77 big chances against them (7th worst in the league). They’ve shown resilience in late periods, scoring 21.05% of their goals between the 31st and 45th minutes and another 21.05% after the 76th minute, but their overall defensive frailties could be exploited by Newcastle’s potent attack.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Streaks
sections.text: Newcastle United have hit the woodwork 14 times this season, ranking 8th in the league for unluckiest teams in this regard. They also rank 5th for headers scored with 10, showcasing aerial prowess that could challenge Ipswich’s backline. Ipswich Town have a worrying streak of conceding in every game during their last 7 head-to-heads against Newcastle. Both teams have a trend of high corner counts, with Ipswich involved in over 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 away matches. Additionally, Newcastle commit a high number of fouls, ranking 18th with 364 fouls this season, which might lead to disciplinary actions under referee Michael Salisbury’s watch.
